arguments.question: Can you describe the nature of your symptoms? For example, are they pain, fatigue, fever, or something else?

clinical_turn.patient_or_tool_response: {'question': 'I’ve been having episodes of wheezing, shortness of breath, and chest tightness that come and go — sometimes worse at night or after exercise. I also cough a lot, especially when exposed to triggers like dust, smoke, or cold air. I’ve noticed my symptoms get worse when I’m stressed or when I’ve had a cold. I’ve also lost most of my sense of smell, and I’ve had trouble breathing after taking ibuprofen or aspirin — sometimes even just a small amount. I’ve had nasal congestion and runny nose for a long time, and I’ve noticed nasal polyps. My asthma symptoms don’t always respond well to usual treatments, and I’ve had to use rescue inhalers more often lately.'}

current_turn_information: I’ve been having episodes of wheezing, shortness of breath, and chest tightness that come and go — sometimes worse at night or after exercise.
I also cough a lot, especially when exposed to triggers like dust, smoke, or cold air.
I’ve noticed my symptoms get worse when I’m stressed or when I’ve had a cold.
I’ve also lost most of my sense of smell, and I’ve had trouble breathing after taking ibuprofen or aspirin — sometimes even just a small amount.
I’ve had nasal congestion and runny nose for a long time, and I’ve noticed nasal polyps.
My asthma symptoms don’t always respond well to usual treatments, and I’ve had to use rescue inhalers more often lately.

case_memory.prior_information_summary: 32-year-old female patient with asthma exacerbation, history of wheezing, shortness of breath, chest tightness, coughing, worse at night and with activity or cold air. Triggers include dust, pet dander, perfumes, viral infections. Lost sense of smell, reacts to aspirin and ibuprofen, has chronic nasal congestion and nasal polyps, asthma hard to control with regular meds. Diagnosed with unspecified asthma with acute exacerbation. Knowledge base includes asthma and aspirin-exacerbated respiratory disease (AERD) information.

current_turn_information: Yes, I’ve been feeling more stressed lately, and it’s been harder to breathe sometimes — especially when I’m anxious or under pressure. I’ve noticed my chest tightness gets worse during those moments, and I’ve had to use my inhaler more often when I’m stressed. I’m not sure if it’s directly causing the symptoms, but it definitely seems to make them worse.

Patient is single, white, English-speaking, with no known allergies or prior asthma diagnosis. Final result: Unspecified asthma with (acute) exacerbation. Knowledge base includes asthma and aspirin-exacerbated respiratory disease (AERD) information.
